card.intuition: A Treap is a BST where each node also carries a random priority; the tree is simultaneously a BST on keys and a min-heap on priorities. Random priorities make rotations happen as if keys were inserted in random order — giving O(log n) expected height without balancing logic.
card.analogy: Like sorting raffle tickets (keys) in alphabetical order but also keeping the lowest ticket numbers (priorities) near the top. The random assignment of priorities ensures the tree never degenerates — no one deliberately can arrange a bad input.
card.time_complexity: O(log n) expected for insert, delete, search; O(log n) split/merge
when_to_use: Ordered set/map when you want simpler code than AVL or Red-Black trees
Split and merge operations on ordered sequences in O(log n)
Implicit treap for sequence problems (index as key)
gotchas: Priority must be random and fixed per node — re-using the same priority breaks the heap invariant
Split(key) produces (left treap with keys ≤ key, right treap with keys > key) in O(log n)
Insert = split at key + create node + merge; Delete = split out the node + merge remaining parts

exercise.prompt: Implement the split operation: split a treap into two treaps (keys ≤ key and keys > key).
exercise.starter_code:
import random

class Node:
    def __init__(self, key):
        self.key      = key
        self.priority = random.random()
        self.left     = None
        self.right    = None

def split(node, key):
    """
    Returns (left, right) where:
      left  contains all nodes with key <= split_key
      right contains all nodes with key >  split_key
    """
    if node is None:
        return None, None
    if node.key <= key:
        # Current node goes to the left treap;
        # split the right subtree further
        left, right = split(node.right, key)
        node.right  = ___________
        return node, right
    else:
        # Current node goes to the right treap;
        # split the left subtree further
        left, right = split(node.left, key)
        node.left   = ___________
        return left, node
hints: When node.key <= key: node stays in the left part; attach the left portion of the right subtree's split as node.right
When node.key > key: node stays in the right part; attach the right portion of the left subtree's split as node.left
